lede: A look at December 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jeremie Frimpong and Roman Červenka, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of December 10–11, 2021
events.desc: A widespread, deadly, and violent tornado outbreak slams the Central, Midwestern, and Southern regions of the United States. Eighty-nine people are killed by the tornadoes, with most of the fatalities occurring in Kentucky, where a single tornado kills 57 people, and injures hundreds of others. Another tornado in Edwardsville, Illinois strikes an Amazon warehouse, killing six workers.

events.title: Constitution of South Africa
events.desc: The new Constitution of South Africa is promulgated by Nelson Mandela.

events.title: Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan
events.desc: Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan establishes the Marriage Fund in the United Arab Emirates in response to rising wedding prices.

events.title: United Nations General Assembly
events.desc: United Nations General Assembly recognizes the Convention against Torture.

events.title: Chinese Civil War
events.desc: Chinese Civil War: The People's Liberation Army begins its siege of Chengdu, the last Kuomintang-held city in mainland China, forcing President of the Republic of China Chiang Kai-shek and his government to retreat to Taiwan.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Royal Navy capital ships HMS Prince of Wales and HMS Repulse are sunk by Imperial Japanese Navy torpedo bombers near British Malaya.

events.title: Brown Dog affair
events.desc: The worst night of the Brown Dog riots in London, when 1,000 medical students, protesting against the existence of a memorial for animals that have been vivisected, clash with 400 police officers.

events.title: Aswan Low Dam
events.desc: The opening of the reservoir of the Aswan Dam in Egypt.

events.title: Nguyễn Trung Trực
events.desc: Forces led by Nguyễn Trung Trực, an anti-colonial guerrilla leader in southern Vietnam, sink the French lorcha L'Esperance.

events.title: Unit of length
events.desc: France adopts the metre as its official unit of length.

births.name: Rasul Guliyev
births.line: Azerbaijani engineer and politician, 22nd Speaker of the National Assembly of Azerbaijan

deaths.text: Jeff Garcia (comedian), American stand-up comedian and voice actor
Srikantadatta Narasimharaja Wadiyar, Indian politician and the titular Maharaja of Mysore
Alfred Nobel, Swedish chemist and engineer, invented Dynamite and founded the Nobel Prize

observances.text: Christian feast day: Behnam, Sarah, and the Forty Martyrs (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Translation of the Holy House of Loreto
